title: Warhammer Quest - Let’s Play - Part 3
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vIAhcX4SppA

Daniel (@dungeondive) faces the aftermath of Part 2's disaster. Harlan the Barbarian is trapped in a guard room with three orcs, his partner Saul the Witch Hunter lost to the bottomless pit. The barbarian manages to kill one orc with a satisfying deathblow, but then a goblin shaman and four goblin spearmen crash the party—an overwhelming force for a lone warrior.

Rather than accept certain death, Daniel invokes the escape rules from the adventure book. Harlan rolls an eight on the escape table, fighting his way out through a gauntlet of orcs and goblins, taking damage but surviving with five hit points. He bursts through a crumbling wall, tumbles down a mountainside, and lands at the feet of Bartok Hammersmith—who has somehow rescued the unconscious Saul. The dwarf demands they complete their quest through an alternate entrance. But there's a narrative twist: Saul's failure to call upon his faith during the bridge crisis costs him dearly. His divine powers are stripped, and he must complete two adventures without any faith tokens. The campaign resets with the same objective but a fresh dungeon layout—a perfect example of how Warhammer Quest's simple mechanics fuel emergent storytelling.

----

Do you prefer strict rules-as-written in solo dungeon crawlers, or do you lean into narrative house rules like Daniel does here?
